How do I write a function to reverse a string in JavaScript?

Here's a simple way to reverse a string in JavaScript:

function reverseString(str) {
  return str.split('').reverse().join('');
}

You can also use a for loop:

function reverseString(str) {
  let reversed = '';
  for (let i = str.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
    reversed += str[i];
  }
  return reversed;
}

Both approaches work, but the first is more concise.

What about using reduce?

Yes! You can use Array.prototype.reduce():

function reverseString(str) {
  return [...str].reduce((reversed, char) => char + reversed, '');
}

This spreads the string into an array of characters and reduces it by prepending each character.
